description_long: This object is a tall, rectangular stone block with a smooth, slightly matte surface and subtle natural veining typical of quarried stone. The block is uniform in color, displaying a light beige to tan hue with faint, organic striations. Its edges are straight and well-defined, with no visible chips or significant wear, suggesting recent fabrication or careful placement. The top face features an engraved or cut-out inscription reading 'Tyler Upshur,' which stands out as the only decorative or identifying mark. The block appears solid and heavy, with no visible seams, joints, or hollow sections. Its overall appearance suggests it is designed for use as a barrier, marker, or boundary element in an urban or landscaped environment.
description: A tall, rectangular beige stone block with smooth surfaces and an engraved 'Tyler Upshur' inscription on top. The block is solid, heavy, and features subtle natural veining.
description_view_0: The front face of the stone block is visible, showing a smooth, uninterrupted surface and the 'Tyler Upshur' inscription on the top edge.
description_view_1: This side view displays another smooth face with the inscription visible in reverse, highlighting the block's uniformity and straight edges.
description_view_2: The rear face is shown, similar to the front but with the inscription reversed and less prominent, emphasizing the block's rectangular geometry.
description_view_3: The opposite side face is visible, again smooth and straight, with the 'Tyler Upshur' inscription clearly readable on the top edge.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a boundary marker or barrier
attribute_installation_method: set in concrete or on a prepared foundation
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering; the surface appears clean and well-maintained with no chips or erosion.
attribute_presence_of_markings_or_engrav: Engraved inscription on the top face reading 'Tyler Upshur'.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: Isolated, likely used as a standalone marker or barrier.
